artifacts.claimText: Northeastern Policy 125 says faculty or staff seeking to use an AI system in University Operations must submit the system and use case for AI Review Committee and Office of Information Security approval when the system processes confidential information, personal information, restricted research data, or may affect legal rights or physical safety.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If the AI System either (i) involves the processing of Confidential Information, Personal Information, or Restricted Research Data or (ii) takes actions that may impact the legal rights or physical safety of an individual: Submit the AI System and its use case for approval by the AI Review Committee; and Submit the AI System and its use case for approval by the Office of Information Security review process.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow faculty/staff University Operations security review claim; source uses mandatory language.

artifacts.claimText: Northeastern Policy 125 says faculty or staff seeking to use an AI system in University Operations or covered outside professional activities must provide required attribution, check AI outputs for accuracy and appropriateness, and validate anti-bias testing when the system processes personal information or affects legal rights or physical safety.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Any faculty or staff member seeking to incorporate the use of an AI System in University Operations or Outside Professional Activities must: Provide appropriate attribution ... Regularly check the AI System's output for accuracy and appropriateness ... If the AI System involves the processing of Personal Information or takes actions that may impact the legal rights or physical safety of an individual, validate that it is regularly tested.

artifacts.claimText: Northeastern research standards say members of the Northeastern community conducting research are expected to follow the university AI Policy, complete AI Review Committee review when research uses an AI system to process confidential information, restricted research data, or personal information, and communicate permitted generative AI uses within project teams based on research activity and sponsor guidelines.

citation.citationTitle: Standards for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Research at Northeastern
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Standards for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Research at Northeastern
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University expects all members of the Northeastern community conducting research to follow the requirements set forth in the university AI Policy and to: Complete the AI Review Committee review process if your research involves using an AI System to process Confidential Information, Restricted Research data or Personal Information ... Follow guidelines set by the funding agency or publisher ... Communicate with fellow lab and project team members about the permitted uses of generative AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Use of AI in Research section
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Northeastern teaching and learning standards state that using generative AI to grade open-ended student responses requires AI Review Committee review because it could affect student legal rights and may involve sensitive personal information and risk of illegal bias or discrimination.

artifacts.claimText: Northeastern administrative AI standards say administrative users should use approved AI environments for data or use cases requiring AI Review Committee approval and should not enter confidential, restricted research, or personal information into an AI system that has not been reviewed and approved by the AI Review Committee and Office of Information Security.

artifacts.claimText: Northeastern's student AI guide advises students to check each syllabus, ask professors when an AI policy is unclear, use AI as a study partner rather than a substitute for their own thinking, and avoid using AI when assignment guidelines direct independent work.

citation.citationTitle: AI at Northeastern: A Student Guide
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI at Northeastern: A Student Guide
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Every class is different ... Check each syllabus carefully. Ask your professors if you're unsure or if an AI policy is unclear ... The key is using AI as a study partner, not as a substitute for your own thinking ... Resist the temptation to use it when assignment guidelines direct you to work independently.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Student guide, Big Ideas and Every class is different sections
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: AI at Northeastern: A Student Guide
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as low-confidence student-facing guidance because PDF extraction was noisy; not treated as binding formal policy.

artifacts.title: Northeastern University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl found official Northeastern sources for Policy 125, research standards, teaching and learning standards, administrative AI standards, and a student guide. Claims are scoped to faculty/staff AI system use, research, teaching, administrative work, and student guidance rather than published as canonical public facts.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The SharePoint approved AI systems list was login-gated and was not fetched or used as evidence.
The student guide PDF extraction was noisy, so its claim is kept as student-facing guidance with lower confidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
